title: Branchial Cleft Anomalies: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Branchial Cleft Anomalies
Identify key diagnostic features of Branchial Cleft Anomalies
Apply clinical reasoning to Branchial Cleft Anomalies management
Recognize common pitfalls in Branchial Cleft Anomalies

sections.title: Overview of Branchial Cleft Anomalies
sections.content:
**Branchial Cleft Anomalies**



**Key Points:**
• 1st cleft: external auditory meatus; anomalies present near ear (preauricular)
• 2nd cleft (most common anomaly): cyst/sinus/fistula anterior to SCM, at angle of mandible
• 2nd cleft fistula: external opening at anterior SCM → courses between internal and external carotid → tonsillar fossa

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• 1st cleft: external auditory meatus; anomalies present near ear (preauricular)
• 2nd cleft (most common anomaly): cyst/sinus/fistula anterior to SCM, at angle of mandible

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• 2nd cleft fistula: external opening at anterior SCM → courses between internal and external carotid → tonsillar fossa
• 3rd/4th cleft: rare, typically left-sided, can present as recurrent thyroiditis or neck abscess
• Treatment: complete surgical excision including fistula tract to prevent recurrence

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Branchial Cleft Anomalies:**

• 2nd cleft fistula: external opening at anterior SCM → courses between internal and external carotid → tonsillar fossa
• 3rd/4th cleft: rare, typically left-sided, can present as recurrent thyroiditis or neck abscess
• Treatment: complete surgical excision including fistula tract to prevent recurrence

options.text: 1st cleft: external auditory meatus; anomalies present near ear (preauricular)
options.explanation: 1st cleft: external auditory meatus; anomalies present near ear (preauricular)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

options.explanation: Treatment: complete surgical excision including fistula tract to prevent recurrence

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases
